paper.title: AIvaluateXR: An Evaluation Framework for on-Device AI in XR with Benchmarking Results
paper.abstract: The deployment of large language models (LLMs) on extended reality (XR) devices has great potential to advance the field of human-AI interaction. In the case of direct, on-device model inference, selecting the appropriate model and device for specific tasks remains challenging. In this paper, we present AIvaluateXR, a comprehensive evaluation framework for benchmarking LLMs running on XR devices. To demonstrate the framework, we deploy 17 selected LLMs across four XR platforms: Magic Leap 2, Met

memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: Peak DRAM usage reduced by 30% after applying compression and quantization

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing model pruning and knowledge distillation can reduce model size by up to 50% without significant accuracy loss
technical_details: NVIDIA's Tensor Cores for matrix multiplication
ARM's Mali-G78 GPU for on-device acceleration
Model pruning and knowledge distillation for size reduction
TensorFlow's quantization and Huffman coding for memory efficiency
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices with moderate optimization efforts. However, the actual bottleneck may be the memory bandwidth requirements, which can be mitigated with proper hardware acceleration and software optimization.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
